name: Permadeath
description: A run failure rule where character defeat or another terminal condition ends the current run and discards most run-scoped progress.
design_notes: Permadeath is less about punishment than about making run decisions matter. The design should separate account progression from run_state_snapshot data, define which rewards survive defeat, and make run_end_reason understandable. Strong implementations make death final for the run while still giving players useful learning, unlock progress, or post-run summary data.
design_purpose: Create high stakes for combat, routing, resource use, and unknown encounters.
Keep runs self-contained so procedural variation and build choices stay meaningful.
Provide a clean boundary for post-run rewards, analytics, replay review, and reset behavior.
player_fantasy: The player feels that every risky room, relic choice, and resource spend matters because the current run can truly end.

parameters.name: run_id
parameters.description: Identifier tying death, rewards, seed logs, and summaries to one run.

parameters.name: run_seed
parameters.description: Seed recorded so failed runs can be reproduced for debugging.

parameters.name: run_end_reason
parameters.description: Terminal outcome that drives summary, rewards, and cleanup rules.

parameters.name: death_persistence_policy
parameters.description: Rule describing which state survives after defeat.

parameters.name: run_state_snapshot
parameters.typical_range: health, inventory, floor, modifiers, seed cursor
parameters.description: Final run data captured before cleanup and reward processing.

parameters.name: meta_currency_id
parameters.description: Account-scoped reward currency granted after eligible outcomes.

parameters.name: save_scum_policy
parameters.description: Restore rule that prevents repeated retries from the same fatal state.

edge_cases: The player disconnects or crashes after lethal damage but before post-run rewards are committed.
A revive, invulnerability frame, or delayed damage tick arrives during the death resolution sequence.
Victory and death conditions happen during the same simulation step.
A save file is restored from a platform cloud backup after the run has already ended.
Co-op runs need to decide whether one player death, full party defeat, or host exit ends the run.
common_bugs: Run-scoped inventory leaks into account state because cleanup runs after reward grants.
Post-run rewards are granted twice when death resolution retries after a network timeout.
The game saves a fatal checkpoint and reloads into an unavoidable death loop.
Abandoned runs are misclassified as deaths and trigger achievements or penalty rules incorrectly.
Seed logs omit rng_stream_id positions, making a reported fatal generation bug impossible to reproduce.
balancing_notes: Permanent run loss is easier to accept when runs are short enough or post-run learning is obvious.
Meta rewards can soften failure, but too much account power can make early-run decisions irrelevant.
Death summaries should identify the chain of causes without making the game feel deterministic or unfair.
accessibility_notes: Provide confirmation and readable consequences for abandoning or deleting a run.
Offer practice, assist, or non-competitive modes if the full run-loss rule blocks players from learning.
Keep post-death summaries readable without requiring players to parse fast combat logs.

implementation_notes.unity: Resolve death through a RunStateManager transaction keyed by run_id. Capture run_state_snapshot, run_end_reason, run_seed, and rng stream cursors before clearing scene objects, then commit meta_currency_id rewards once with an idempotency token.
implementation_notes.godot: Keep run state in an autoload or saved Resource separate from account progression. When health reaches the fatal threshold, lock new input, emit a run_ended signal, write the death_persistence_policy result, and delete or archive the exit-save slot.
implementation_notes.unreal: Use a GameInstance or subsystem boundary between account data and run data. Server-authoritative runs should resolve death on the server, replicate the final run_end_reason, and guard reward grants against repeated RPCs or reconnect attempts.
implementation_notes.web: Represent death as an immutable run event in IndexedDB or local storage. Store run_id, run_seed, run_end_reason, and reward grant ids together so reloads can resume the summary screen without resurrecting invalid run state.

example_games.title: Spelunky
example_games.note: Ends a run on death and restarts from a fresh run state.

example_games.title: The Binding of Isaac
example_games.note: Uses run death as a reset boundary while account unlocks can persist.
